verdict: Spend collapsed to near zero this period; without lead tracking, we can't prove the $1,608 worked.
diagnosis: Facebook spend totaled $1,608 over 28 days but daily spend dropped to $2.77 — well below the $10/day learning threshold.

signals.headline: Total spend $1,608 over 28 days
signals.detail: One active Facebook interest campaign carried all paid activity this period.

signals.headline: Latest daily spend just $2.77
signals.detail: Daily pacing has nearly stopped. Algorithm cannot optimize at this level.

signals.headline: Zero lead or conversion data tracked
signals.detail: No pipeline, organic, or local data flowing. Cannot calculate cost per lead this period.

signals.headline: Average $18.70 per day in period
signals.detail: Below the $30/day threshold most studios need for consistent lead flow.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.body: <cite index="3-22">Fitness averages $29.70 cost per lead on Meta in 2026.</cite> <cite index="3-8,3-9,3-10">Budget gyms run $8–$15, mid-range $15–$30, boutique studios $25–$50.</cite>

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.body: <cite index="3-30,3-31,3-32">Fitness cost per lead bottoms near $13.50 in April and May before spiking to $48 in July.</cite> This period sits inside the cheapest lead window of the year.

competitor.label: Competitor
competitor.body: <cite index="14-2,14-3">Pilates and reformer classes keep growing on demand for low-impact, strength-building workouts.</cite> <cite index="14-14">Pilates now represents over 43% of primary studio modalities.</cite>

recommendations.title: Restore daily spend to at least $30
recommendations.detail: <cite index="8-9">The starting sweet spot for local fitness studios is roughly $30 per day, or $900 per month.</cite> Current $2.77/day starves the algorithm.

recommendations.title: Install lead tracking before scaling further
recommendations.detail: Without lead counts or pipeline data, we cannot tell if spend is working. Consider Meta instant forms plus a simple intake log before increasing budget.

recommendations.title: Test a Pilates or hybrid class hook
recommendations.detail: <cite index="14-1,14-2,14-3">Pilates and reformer-based classes continue to grow on demand for low-impact, strength-building workouts.</cite> Worth testing as a creative angle against the current interest targeting.
